results.title: SKIM - pdf option - Other Software & Development - Literature & Latte Forums
results.content:
# SKIM - pdf option

In another post, Simon pointed out that Skim is a free alternative to Preview.

Give Skim a try. It has a better annotation system than Preview, and yet is way lighter than Abode (you’d need the pro version anyway). And it’s free. I’ve made it my default pdf reader instead of Preview.

Thanks Simon - a really excellent tip. I have done the same.

skim-app.sourceforge.net/

There is a useful Wiki page on the application here:

skim-app.sourceforge.net/wiki/in … /Main\_Page

:slight_smile:

:slight_smile:

Cheers LL, my pleasure. A few things I like about Skim:

results.title: Download Skim for Mac | MacUpdate
results.content:
Skim is a lightweight, easy-to-use PDF reader that is perfect for students, researchers, and anyone who needs to read and annotate PDFs. It has a clean and intuitive interface, and it supports a wide range of features, including: Viewing, zooming, and scrolling through PDFs Adding and editing notes Highlighting text Creating bookmarks Searching for text Exporting notes and annotations Skim is also highly customizable, and you can change the font, font size, and other settings to make it look and feel the way you want. Overall, Skim is a great choice for anyone who needs a simple, reliable PDF reader. It is free to download and use, and it is available for macOS. Here are some of the pros and cons of Skim: Pros: Free and open source Lightweight and easy to use Supports a wide range of [...] View older Skim versions

I used to always use PDF Expert. But it became increasingly sluggish, so I looked around for alternatives and ended up with Skim. I am absolutely thrilled. I don't miss any features that are important to me, and Skim is lightning fast. In the time it used to take me to wait for the other app to start up, I can now open 10 large PDF files with Skim. It is free to download and use, and it is available for macOS.

results.title: Why I left Preview and Started using Skim for Reading Research Papers – Amit Seal Ami
results.content:
### Alternatives I tried

 Foxit PDF Reader - Honestly, it would be my go to choice if I did not find Skim. The only issue I find is that I can not change the color presets, i.e. you either have to use their color preset, or have to change color manually for each highlight
 Adobe PDF Reader - Slow, sidebar takes too much space, color change tedious, did not find color presets
 PDF Expert Free - Does not allow saving annotated PDF in free version
 PDFReader Pro Lite - Looks like and feels like Skim PDF, and has advertisements. Interestingly, it also was able to render notes I made using Skim.

## SKIM - The PDF Reader I chose

Skim is an open source, free PDF reader and annotator that’s built for OSX. Here are the advantages due to which I use Skim: [...] Amit Seal Ami 

# Amit Seal Ami

Blog of Amit Seal Ami

# Why I left Preview and Started using Skim for Reading Research Papers

Written on September 8, 2020

## Problems in OSX Preview for PDF

Preview is good and all for reading PDF. I admit that its user interface is really sleek, beautiful. But (there is always a but) it is bad when you try to use it for highlighting PDFs and writing notes, which is really important when it comes to research. That’s exactly where I found three issues which forced me to stop using it and choose Skim - an open source, free PDF reader instead.

### Highlight Messes up Selection Tool

results.title: Skin for Mac: Pros, Cons, and Top Alternatives
results.content:
## Part 3: How Much Does Skim PDF Reader Cost for Mac Users?

Skim is a good choice for Mac users looking for a good PDF reader, especially because it's free. It's the first open-source PDF reader developed just for macOS, which means you don't have to pay anything to use it.

Since Skim is open-source, it's regularly updated by a community of developers. They add new features and improvements without you having to pay for upgrades or licenses.

## Part 4: Pros and Cons of Skim PDF Reader for Mac

## Part 5: Skim PDF Reader for Mac OS Alternatives

Skim for Mac is great for handling PDFs, but there are other good options. Let's look at some other tools Mac users can try, each with its special features.

### 1. PDFelement
